--- SOURCE 4: What is Competency-Based Education? ---
URL: https://www.aacnnursing.org/essentials/tool-kit/competency-based-education

SUMMARY:
*   About AACN Show sub-menu for About AACN AACN defines competency-based education as “a system of instruction, assessment, feedback, self-reflection, and academic reporting that is based on students demonstrating that they have learned the knowledge, attitudes, motivations, self-perceptions, and skills expected of them as they progress through their education.” Other organizations define competency-based education differently, including the U.S. Department of Education, which more narrowly defines this term as education “that organizes academic content according to competencies—what a student knows and can do—rather than following a more traditional scheme, such as by course.” Nursing programs are encouraged to institute a process to ensure curricula address the competencies delineated in the Essentials and assess student achievement of those competencies.

FULL CONTENT:
What is Competency-Based Education? 

===============

SUMMARY:
By reinforcing a competency-based approach, schools can identify specific areas where students may struggle and tailor interventions that

IMAGE 1:
URL: https://prepwithharshita.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/White-and-Pink-Doodle-Handwritten-Mind-Map-Brainstorm-1024x576.png
DESCRIPTION: The image outlines the tenets of the Competency-Based Curriculum in Zambia, including mastery of specific skills, developing assessments, designing instructions, and implementing and evaluating, accompanied by an illustrative icon of books, a graduation cap, and a small model of a government or educational institution.

IMAGE 2:
URL: https://image.slidesharecdn.com/cbcpresentation-161205034443/95/competencybased-curriculum-development-method-teaching-3-638.jpg?cb=1480909524
DESCRIPTION: A diagram that explains the competency based TVET framework and the different stages of the process

IMAGE 3:
URL: https://static.wixstatic.com/media/5ceb6b_f8edef71b80843d4af007e3193212501~mv2.jpg/v1/fill/w_560,h_1400,al_c,q_85,enc_auto/5ceb6b_f8edef71b80843d4af007e3193212501~mv2.jpg
DESCRIPTION: Three individuals are collaboratively assembling a large puzzle piece, symbolizing the integration of competency-based learning initiatives.

IMAGE 4:
URL: https://www.hurix.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/competency-based-curriculum-768x852.jpg
DESCRIPTION: The image outlines six key tenets of developing a competency-based curriculum, including identifying competencies, organizing them into themes, establishing performance criteria, creating learning experiences, assessing competency, and evaluating the curriculum's effectiveness.

IMAGE 5:
URL: https://image.slidesharecdn.com/cbcpresentation-161205034443/95/competencybased-curriculum-development-method-teaching-4-638.jpg?cb=1480909524
DESCRIPTION: The image illustrates the relationship between the components of competency standard and competency-based curriculum, comparing elements like unit of competency, descriptor, and criteria with corresponding course titles, descriptions, levels, and assessment methods.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

enriched_points.expanded_content: Governments should actively endorse and promote Competency-Based Curriculum (CBC) initiatives, ensuring alignment with educational goals and the needs of diverse student populations.
Allocate sufficient funding to develop and implement comprehensive training programs for educators, equipping them with necessary skills for effective CBC delivery and assessment.

Foster an environment that encourages innovative teaching methodologies and practices, allowing educators to creatively engage students through personalized and competency-focused learning experiences.
